negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing full invariant: block verification does not hard-fail RitualWallet balance C9 failures unless skip mode is enabled; instead underfunded wallet cases are allowed for Scheduler.execute contract eviction.
related_but_insufficient_code: Parent-state Scheduler-backed verification is present and local pool membership appears absent, but C9 wallet balance is explicitly allowed for contract eviction and no skip-mode gate was found.

notes: Confirmed gap because one required behavior is intentionally not enforced.
rationale: The lineage shows a temporary non-fatal/local-pool policy that was later superseded by current parent-state scheduled verification, with skip mode as the remaining explicit bypass.
statement: Current block verification for scheduled transactions must ignore local scheduled/async pool membership and validate block-contained scheduled transactions against parent-block Scheduler and RitualWallet contract state; C1-C10 failures hard-fail block verification unless the explicit validity-constraints skip mode is enabled.
suggested_falsification_tests: Verify the same block on nodes with different local scheduled pools but identical parent Scheduler state and assert both reach the same scheduled-verification result.

Mutate parent Scheduler state or scheduled transaction metadata to violate a C1-C10 constraint and assert block verification returns ScheduledConstraintViolation with skip mode disabled and only logs/continues with skip mode enabled.
summary: Scheduled verification is parent-state backed, but RitualWallet C9 hard-fail behavior is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Construct valid scheduled tx with insufficient RitualWallet balance and skip mode disabled; invariant expects verifier failure, current port tests/logic predict Ok.
title: Scheduled verification is parent-state backed
